brand.name: FreshCo
brand.guidelines: Tone: confident, restrained intensity — never loud or gimmicky. Visuals evoke the calm before the whistle: tunnel walks, floodlit warm-ups, mist rising off a freshly cut pitch at dusk. Primary palette is deep forest green with sharp neon-lime highlights; warm off-white and near-black anchor typography. Backgrounds should lean into cinematic sports atmospheres: rain-slicked surfaces, stadium tunnel light spill, dewy grass at golden hour, condensation and cool textures. Lighting must feel editorial — directional, moody, with intentional shadow play. Typography: geometric sans-serif, uppercase headlines, generous letter-spacing. Avoid all hydration clichés ('quench', 'refresh', 'splash'). No discount language. Celebrate quiet discipline — early pitch sessions, ice baths, match-day routines — never glorify pain. No competitor branding or stock-photo aesthetics.

products.name: ProFlow 750 Insulated Bottle
products.description: Double-wall stainless bottle that keeps drinks cold for 24 hours — built for training sessions, match days, and long rides

products.name: ChargeUp Electrolyte Mix — Citrus
products.description: Low-sugar hydration powder with sodium, potassium, and magnesium — mix with water for fast replenishment after sweat-heavy workouts

target_audience: Professional and semi-professional footballers across the English pyramid — from the EFL and National League to ambitious academy pros — ages roughly 18–35, who train most days, play a congested fixture schedule, and prioritise hydration, electrolytes, and recovery between matches and travel
campaign_message: From the training pitch to matchday — hydrate with purpose, recover with discipline. Built for the full ninety.
